data.description:
## Why
CLAUDE.md's 'Gate protocols (MANDATORY)' section (CLAUDE.md:112-121) instructs agents that even on a PASS they 'must remediate all valid advisory findings before continuing'. Field data: ~13 advisories/round, 95% of remediation edits mint new findings, and the mandated loop drove the 4+-round tail that consumed 60% of gate compute. With the convergence floors now default-ON (change 562) the operator wants to LOOSEN this guidance — NOT replace it with guidance to ignore advisories — and observe whether agents implement advisories properly on their own judgment.

## What
- Edit CLAUDE.md's plan-review protocol paragraph (~lines 112-121): remove the MUST-remediate-all-valid-advisories mandate. Replace with neutral judgment framing, e.g.: review the advisory findings and coaching notes; apply the ones that genuinely improve the plan; a blocking verdict must still be remediated and re-reviewed. Do NOT add language discouraging advisory uptake.
- Sweep for echoes of the mandate: docs/plan-review-gate.md protocol prose, CONTRIBUTING.md, and the completion-verifier protocol paragraph stays UNCHANGED (close FAILs remain mandatory-remediate). The 'Sign on every round' and BLOCK-must-remediate guidance stays verbatim.
- Record the observation plan on the epic: compare advisory-uptake rate and rounds-to-pass before/after via REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (norm_id survival across rounds = an advisory was addressed).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLAUDE.md no longer contains a mandate to remediate all valid advisory findings; the replacement framing is judgment-based and does not discourage acting on advisories.
- [ ] BLOCK-remediation and sign-every-round guidance are unchanged.
- [ ] No other repo doc still states the mandatory-advisory-remediation rule (grep for 'remediate' + 'advisory' across CLAUDE.md, docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [ ] make lint passes on the change.
- [ ] make test passes on the change.
data.title: Loosen the mandatory advisory-remediation guidance in CLAUDE.md (observe organic advisory uptake)
